summary: Whitney Wolfe Herd believes that traditional dating app mechanics—relying on photos and strict criteria—cause people to miss out on compatible partners because humans struggle to articulate what they truly need. To solve this, she is developing an AI-powered matchmaker designed to understand users better than they understand themselves.

principles.statement: People cannot accurately articulate what they need in a relationship.
principles.rationale: When users rely solely on a photo and a rigid list of criteria, they often filter out highly compatible partners—just as Wolfe Herd admits she would have filtered out her own husband.
principles.source_quote: We can’t articulate what we need in a relationship

principles.statement: AI can understand us better than we understand ourselves.
principles.rationale: Because humans struggle to identify their true relationship needs, an emotionally intelligent AI can step in to make better matchmaking choices for us than we would make for ourselves.

mental_models.name: AI as an Emotionally Intelligent Matchmaker
mental_models.description: Viewing artificial intelligence not merely as a sorting algorithm for user preferences, but as an emotionally intelligent entity capable of understanding deep human relationship needs better than the users themselves.
mental_models.example: Consulting expert psychologists and relationship counselors to program an AI that chooses matches for users based on a deeper understanding of their psychology.

signature_quotes.text: the world’s smartest and most emotionally intelligent matchmaker in existence
signature_quotes.context: Describing the ultimate goal of her new AI-powered matchmaking project at Bumble.

anti_patterns.description: Relying exclusively on a photo and a rigid list of criteria to evaluate a potential romantic partner.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: People are poor at articulating what they actually need in a relationship, meaning they will likely reject someone who could be a highly compatible life partner.
